cases.synopsis: Marcus T. is a 15-year-old referred by his school because of long-standing academic and daily-living concerns. Since early elementary school, he has struggled to keep up with age-level learning. He reads at approximately a second- to third-grade level and needs new information broken into simple steps and repeated many times. Even with support and extra time, he often forgets instructions from one day to the next.

hotspots.title: Patient Interview

hotspots.detail: Marcus has struggled since early elementary school to keep up with age-level learning. He reads at approximately a second- to third-grade level and needs new information broken into simple steps and repeated many times.

hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: School and intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Even with support and extra time, Marcus often forgets instructions from one day to the next. These difficulties have been present since childhood and occur across school, home, and community settings.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: Socially, Marcus is friendly but struggles to follow conversations, understand jokes or sarcasm, and participate independently.

hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: At home, Marcus requires regular assistance with age-expected tasks. He can dress himself and complete basic hygiene routines, but he often forgets steps unless reminded.

cases.presentingConcern: Persistent grief, avoidance, and declining functioning after her husband's death
cases.referralSource: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.
cases.primaryGoal: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.
cases.synopsis: Renee M. is a 52-year-old Latina woman whose husband died 22 months ago. She has tried to focus on other things in her life, like tending her rose garden and taking pottery classes. She has continued to experience intense yearning, difficulty accepting the death, avoidance of reminders, and withdrawal from social and meaningful activities for the past 18 months.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Renee says she has continued to experience intense yearning and difficulty accepting her husband's death.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: Intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Her husband's death occurred 22 months ago, and the grief-related symptoms have continued for the past 18 months.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: Renee shows avoidance of reminders and withdrawal from social and meaningful activities.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Renee has tried to focus on other parts of her life, including tending her rose garden and taking pottery classes.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Renee has always looked forward to working because she enjoys being a nurse, but she has been managing a significant decline in work functioning.

hotspots.label: Environmental Observation
hotspots.title: Environmental Observation
hotspots.summary: Environmental record review.
hotspots.detail: Her Labrador keeps her company and yet is a daily reminder of her husband's death.

cases.presentingConcern: Chronic sadness, low energy, and low self-worth

cases.synopsis: Maria L. is a 47-year-old Latina woman who has spent the past three and a half years living with what she describes as a constant heaviness, a chronic sense of sadness woven into her daily routine. Working as a library assistant, she moves through most days on autopilot, battling low energy, poor appetite, and a steady undercurrent of low self-worth while even small decisions take outsized effort.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Maria describes a constant heaviness and a chronic sense of sadness that has been woven into her daily routine for the past three and a half years.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: Intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: She reports low energy, poor appetite, and a steady undercurrent of low self-worth. Even small decisions take outsized effort.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: Maria moves through most days on autopilot and continues to meet family and work responsibilities, but the emotional weight never fully lifts.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: She cannot recall any stretch longer than a few weeks during which the mood lifted completely, and she denies ever having periods of distinctly elevated mood or increased goal-directed energy.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.

hotspots.label: Environmental Observation
hotspots.title: Environmental Observation
hotspots.summary: Environmental record review.
hotspots.detail: Outside of work, Maria tends a small tomato garden and listens to mystery audiobooks in the evenings, often realizing she has zoned out and needs to replay chapters.

cases.presentingConcern: Restricting, binge eating, compensatory behaviors, and weight-related preoccupation
cases.referralSource: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.
cases.primaryGoal: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.
cases.synopsis: Jordan K. is a 22-year-old college student who sought help after becoming increasingly concerned about eating habits, weight, and health over the past year. Jordan reports trying to eat clean and often starts the day with the intention of limiting food intake to stay in control.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Jordan reports trying to eat clean and often starts the day with the intention of limiting food intake to stay in control, sometimes by skipping meals or restricting certain foods believed to lead to weight gain.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: Intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Several times per week for the past eight months, Jordan has experienced repeated episodes of eating large amounts of food in a short period of time, usually in the evening when alone, with a sense of being unable to stop.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: Jordan describes these eating episodes as feeling disconnected or on autopilot and says they continue even when physically uncomfortable.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Afterward, Jordan experiences intense guilt, embarrassment, and fear of gaining weight. Jordan regularly engages in self-induced vomiting and, less frequently, uses laxatives. On other days, Jordan compensates by fasting, exercising for long periods, or trying to strictly control food intake again.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Friends have noticed that Jordan sometimes avoids meals with others or excuses themselves shortly after eating, though Jordan has largely kept these behaviors hidden.

hotspots.label: Environmental Observation
hotspots.title: Environmental Observation
hotspots.summary: Environmental record review.
hotspots.detail: Jordan's weight has remained within a typical range, but there is significant preoccupation with body shape and fear of weight gain. These behaviors interfere with concentration in class, social events that involve food, and day-to-day functioning. There is no evidence of a medical condition explaining the symptoms, and Jordan does not report episodes of overeating without attempts to compensate.

cases.presentingConcern: Chronic social discomfort, odd beliefs, and suspiciousness

cases.synopsis: Jonah R. is a 29-year-old White man who has struggled for the past several years to maintain close relationships, often feeling like he does not fit in with others. He spends most evenings alone reading about astrology and obscure spiritual systems, believing that certain planetary alignments allow him to sense others' intentions.

hotspots.label: Patient Interview
hotspots.title: Patient Interview
hotspots.summary: Interview notes from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Jonah reports that certain planetary alignments allow him to sense others' intentions. He says he sometimes feels subtle messages in the way streetlights flicker or in overheard fragments of conversation, though he acknowledges he may be reading too much into things.

hotspots.label: Intake Chart
hotspots.title: Intake Chart
hotspots.summary: Intake information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: For the past several years, Jonah has struggled to maintain close relationships and often feels like he does not fit in with others.

hotspots.label: Observation Notes
hotspots.title: Observation Notes
hotspots.summary: Behavioral observations described in the vignette.
hotspots.detail: Coworkers describe Jonah as socially awkward, and he avoids office gatherings because he feels people can sense his anxiety.

hotspots.label: Medication / History Record
hotspots.title: Medication / History Record
hotspots.summary: History information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: Jonah has no history of psychosis, but he experiences chronic social discomfort, odd speech patterns, and persistent suspicion that others judge him harshly.

hotspots.label: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.title: Family or Collateral Note
hotspots.summary: Collateral information from the case record.
hotspots.detail: No additional information is available in this portion of the case record.

hotspots.label: Environmental Observation
hotspots.title: Environmental Observation
hotspots.summary: Environmental record review.
hotspots.detail: Jonah spends most evenings alone reading about astrology and obscure spiritual systems. He enjoys sketching fantasy landscapes and caring for his pet gecko.
